description: Investigation of LLMs' struggle with basic character counting tasks (e.g., counting 'r' in 'strawberry'). Evaluates multiple advanced LLMs including GPT-4o, Llama-3, Qwen-1.5, Gemma-1, Phi-3, Mistral-v0.3, DeepSeek-V2, InternLM, and Yi-1.5. Tests various conjectures about tokenization, architecture, and training data as causes for counting failures. Demonstrates that engaging reasoning mechanisms is most robust approach for improving performance. Challenges prevalent assumptions about inherent LLM deficiencies and highlights importance of reasoning-before-responding during model training. Published at NAACL 2025.

description: Comprehensive human evaluation study of 10 LLMs across different pretraining methods, prompts, and model scales for news summarization. Key findings: (1) Instruction tuning, not model size, is crucial for zero-shot summarization capability; (2) Previous studies underestimated human performance due to low-quality references. Conducted human evaluation using high-quality summaries from freelance writers. Found LLM summaries comparable to human-written summaries despite stylistic differences in paraphrasing. Evaluated on CNN/DailyMail and XSUM datasets. Demonstrates importance of instruction tuning for summarization tasks and challenges existing evaluation paradigms with better human baselines.

description: XL-Sum: Largest multilingual abstractive summarization dataset with 1 million professionally annotated article-summary pairs from BBC across 44 languages. Covers low to high-resource languages with highly abstractive, concise summaries. Portuguese: 71k+ pairs (ROUGE-1=37.17, ROUGE-L=28.56). Swahili: 9,872 pairs (ROUGE-1=37.67, ROUGE-2=17.85, ROUGE-L=30.91). Tamil: 20k+ pairs (ROUGE-1=24.33, ROUGE-2=11.06, ROUGE-L=22.07). Fine-tuned mT5 achieving >11 ROUGE-2 scores on 10 languages, with some exceeding 15 through multilingual training. Demonstrates competitive performance for low-resource summarization. Standard benchmark for multilingual news summarization research. XL-Sum addresses the limited availability of datasets for low/mid-resource languages in text summarization, featuring highly abstractive, concise, and high-quality summaries validated through human and intrinsic evaluation. The benchmark demonstrates competitive performance with mT5 fine-tuning, achieving over 11 ROUGE-2 scores across 10 languages and exceeding 15 on some through multilingual training.

description: WikiLingua: Large-scale multilingual benchmark dataset for cross-lingual abstractive summarization extracted from WikiHow collaborative how-to guides across 18 languages including English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, German, Russian, Italian, Indonesian, Dutch, Arabic, Vietnamese, Chinese, Thai, Japanese, Korean, Hindi, Czech, and Turkish. Dataset features gold-standard article-summary alignments across languages using image alignment methodology. Research evaluates cross-lingual summarization approaches including direct crosslingual methods without translation at inference time, leveraging synthetic data and Neural Machine Translation for pre-training. Baseline comparisons include traditional pipeline approaches (Summarize→Translate, Translate→Summarize) and advanced multilingual models (mT5, mBART-50) with both monolingual and cross-lingual fine-tuning strategies. WikiLingua Enhanced provides enhanced methodologies for cross-lingual transfer learning, evaluating various approaches including fine-tuned mBART models, Lead-3 baselines, and pipeline methods across multiple language pairs. The study demonstrates improved performance through enhanced cross-lingual training techniques, addressing challenges in cross-lingual summarization such as maintaining content quality across language boundaries while ensuring semantic preservation and factual accuracy. Evaluation uses automatic metrics (ROUGE, BLEU) with human evaluation studies, demonstrating significant performance improvements for direct cross-lingual approaches while maintaining cost efficiency during inference.

The study constructs a high-quality evaluation dataset based on guidelines emphasizing accuracy, coverage, and understandability, with crowd-sourced summaries filtered through human ratings. Results highlight limitations of the ROUGE metric overlooked in monolingual summarization, providing insights into cross-lingual summarization challenges and evaluation methodologies.

description: XTREME (Cross-lingual TRansfer Evaluation of Multilingual Encoders) is a comprehensive multi-task benchmark for evaluating cross-lingual generalization capabilities of multilingual representations across 40 languages and 9 tasks. description: Critical analysis of multilingual machine translation benchmarks focusing on four languages (Asante Twi, Japanese, Jinghpaw, and South Azerbaijani) that uncovers shortcomings in FLORES+ benchmark quality. The study reveals that many translations fall below claimed quality standards, with source sentences often being domain-specific and culturally biased toward English-speaking contexts, advocating for more domain-general and culturally neutral evaluation approaches.

description: No Language Left Behind (NLLB-200) represents a groundbreaking universal machine translation system designed to eradicate language barriers on a global scale by supporting over 200 languages. The project addresses the critical challenge of serving the vast majority of low-resource languages that have been left behind by previous MT efforts. NLLB-200 employs a Sparsely Gated Mixture of Experts conditional compute model trained with novel data mining techniques specifically tailored for low-resource languages. The system achieves a 44% BLEU improvement relative to previous state-of-the-art, evaluated across over 40,000 translation directions using the human-translated Flores-200 benchmark. The project prioritizes ethical considerations and translation safety through comprehensive toxicity assessment across all supported languages, laying crucial groundwork towards realizing truly universal translation capabilities.

NLLB-200 employs a Sparsely Gated Mixture of Experts conditional compute model trained with novel data mining techniques specifically tailored for low-resource languages. The system achieves a 44% BLEU improvement relative to previous state-of-the-art, evaluated across over 40,000 translation directions using the human-translated Flores-200 benchmark. The project prioritizes ethical considerations and translation safety through comprehensive toxicity assessment across all supported languages, laying crucial groundwork towards realizing truly universal translation capabilities.

models: M2M-100 (various sizes)
description: M2M-100 represents a breakthrough in moving 'Beyond English-Centric Multilingual Machine Translation' by creating a true Many-to-Many multilingual translation model capable of translating directly between any pair of 100 languages. Unlike previous approaches that relied heavily on English-centric training data, M2M-100 addresses worldwide translation needs through a training dataset covering thousands of language directions with supervised data created through large-scale mining. The model combines dense scaling with language-specific sparse parameters to achieve high quality translations, delivering gains of more than 10 BLEU when directly translating between non-English directions while remaining competitive with the best single systems in WMT evaluations.

The model combines dense scaling with language-specific sparse parameters to achieve high quality translations, delivering gains of more than 10 BLEU when directly translating between non-English directions while remaining competitive with the best single systems in WMT evaluations.

description: Expansion of FLORES+ benchmark for Portuguese-Emakhuwa machine translation evaluation, addressing low-resource language needs in Mozambique. The work involved translating dev and devtest sets from Portuguese to Emakhuwa with rigorous quality assurance including post-editing and adequacy assessments. The study provides baseline results from neural MT training and multilingual model fine-tuning, highlighting challenges with spelling inconsistencies in Emakhuwa and establishing foundations for future research.

models: neural MT models, Google, Facebook M2M
description: MENYO-20k presents the first multi-domain parallel corpus for Yorùbá-English machine translation with standardized train-test splits and clean orthography. The research analyzes the effect of domain and diacritics in Yorùbá-English neural machine translation, comparing custom models against massively multilingual systems like Google and Facebook M2M. The study demonstrates significant improvements (+8.7 BLEU over Google, +9.1 BLEU over Facebook M2M) when translating to Yorùbá, establishing high-quality benchmarks for African language machine translation.

description: WMT-24 shared task evaluating machine translation quality and quality estimation across multiple language pairs. Also evaluates MT quality and quality estimation for Korean-English and Spanish-English pairs. GPT-4o achieves highest translation quality scores and quality estimation (Korean En-Ko: Pearson .796, Spanish En-Es: .757). Claude-3.5 competitive across tasks. Systematic comparison of closed vs. open LLMs using prompt-based DA scoring framework.

description: Comprehensive multilingual LLM evaluation benchmark comparing 10 open-source models across Bengali and English. Evaluates 8 translated benchmark datasets spanning Commonsense (Hellaswag, Winogrande, CommonsenseQA, BoolQ, OpenBookQA), Science (ARC), Math (GSM8K), and Multidomain (MMLU) categories. Bengali datasets translated using GPT-4o-mini with comprehensive translation pipeline. Systematic analysis reveals consistent performance gaps for Bengali compared to English, particularly for smaller models and Mistral family. DeepSeek shows promising robustness maintaining stable cross-language performance. Includes detailed tokenization analysis showing Bengali requires significantly more tokens per instance and per word compared to English.

description: HindiLLM: Comprehensive Hindi-English bidirectional machine translation evaluation using autoregressive language models trained from scratch on 37.34GB Hindi corpus. HindiLLM-Medium achieves mean scores of 2.35 (En→Hi) and 2.03 (Hi→En) on 0-4 human evaluation scale. Includes custom HindiLLM tokenizer with 50K vocabulary optimized for Devanagari script, reducing token count by 55% compared to GPT-2 tokenizer. Models evaluated on 1.49M sentence pairs from Hindi-English parallel corpus with human evaluation criteria: 4=Excellent, 3=Good, 2=Understandable, 1=Barely understandable, 0=Incomprehensible. Also evaluated on classification, NLI, sentiment analysis tasks showing superior performance over multilingual models.

description: Comprehensive Hindi LLM evaluation suite introducing five new Hindi benchmark datasets: IFEval-Hi (instruction following), MT-Bench-Hi (conversation), GSM8K-Hi (mathematical reasoning), ChatRAG-Hi (retrieval-augmented QA), and BFCL-Hi (fact checking). Created using human annotation combined with translate-and-verify methodology to preserve linguistic and cultural nuances missing in direct English translations. Provides extensive benchmarking of open-source LLMs supporting Hindi with detailed comparative analysis of current capabilities. Methodology serves as replicable framework for developing benchmarks in other low-resource languages.

description: Congolese Swahili-French bidirectional neural machine translation for humanitarian response. Created 25,302-sentence parallel corpus for training. description: MENYO-20k presents the first multi-domain parallel corpus for Yorùbá-English machine translation with standardized train-test splits and clean orthography. The research analyzes the effect of domain and diacritics in Yorùbá-English neural machine translation, comparing custom models against massively multilingual systems like Google and Facebook M2M. The study demonstrates significant improvements (+8.7 BLEU over Google, +9.1 BLEU over Facebook M2M) when translating to Yorùbá, establishing high-quality benchmarks for African language machine translation.

description: Foundational research on universal machine translation using the Transformer architecture, establishing key principles for multilingual neural machine translation systems. The work focuses on training universal models capable of handling multiple language pairs simultaneously, with evaluation on FLORES datasets for English-German and English-French translation pairs, contributing to the development of modern multilingual MT architectures and laying groundwork for subsequent universal translation systems.

description: Research on topic classification as a proxy for machine translation evaluation, examining the effectiveness of different translation systems (GPT-4, Seamless M4T, human translators) across multiple languages including English, Russian, Chinese, and Hindi. The study explores novel evaluation methodologies for machine translation quality assessment through downstream task performance in topic classification scenarios, providing insights into how translation quality affects downstream NLP task performance.

description: TituLLMs: First pretrained Bangla LLMs (1B and 3B parameters) adapted from Llama-3.2 with comprehensive machine translation evaluation. Evaluates MT and reasoning with custom Bangla datasets including Bangla MMLU (87,869 entries), BoolQ BN (1,976 entries), CommonsenseQA BN (10,962 entries), OpenBookQA BN (5,944 entries), and PIQA BN (17,177 entries). BLEU scores up to 0.57, SacreBLEU up to 49.5, SQM up to 5.07. Features Bengali tokenizer adaptation, Expressive Semantic Translation (EST) methods, and comprehensive benchmarking across knowledge and reasoning tasks.

description: Comprehensive multilingual LLM evaluation benchmark comparing 10 open-source models across Bengali and English. Evaluates 8 translated benchmark datasets spanning Commonsense (Hellaswag, Winogrande, CommonsenseQA, BoolQ, OpenBookQA), Science (ARC), Math (GSM8K), and Multidomain (MMLU) categories. Bengali datasets translated using GPT-4o-mini with comprehensive translation pipeline. Systematic analysis reveals consistent performance gaps for Bengali compared to English, particularly for smaller models and Mistral family. DeepSeek shows promising robustness maintaining stable cross-language performance. Includes detailed tokenization analysis showing Bengali requires significantly more tokens per instance and per word compared to English.

description: HindiLLM: Comprehensive Hindi-English bidirectional machine translation evaluation using autoregressive language models trained from scratch on 37.34GB Hindi corpus. HindiLLM-Medium achieves mean scores of 2.35 (En→Hi) and 2.03 (Hi→En) on 0-4 human evaluation scale. Includes custom HindiLLM tokenizer with 50K vocabulary optimized for Devanagari script, reducing token count by 55% compared to GPT-2 tokenizer. Models evaluated on 1.49M sentence pairs from Hindi-English parallel corpus with human evaluation criteria: 4=Excellent, 3=Good, 2=Understandable, 1=Barely understandable, 0=Incomprehensible. Also evaluated on classification, NLI, sentiment analysis tasks showing superior performance over multilingual models.

description: Comprehensive Hindi LLM evaluation suite introducing five new Hindi benchmark datasets: IFEval-Hi (instruction following), MT-Bench-Hi (conversation), GSM8K-Hi (mathematical reasoning), ChatRAG-Hi (retrieval-augmented QA), and BFCL-Hi (fact checking). Created using human annotation combined with translate-and-verify methodology to preserve linguistic and cultural nuances missing in direct English translations. Provides extensive benchmarking of open-source LLMs supporting Hindi with detailed comparative analysis of current capabilities. Methodology serves as replicable framework for developing benchmarks in other low-resource languages.

description: AfriMTE: High-quality human evaluation dataset for African languages machine translation with simplified MQM guidelines for error detection and direct assessment scoring. AfriCOMET: State-of-the-art MT evaluation metrics for African languages achieving 0.441 Spearman-rank correlation with human judgments. Created evaluation data for 13 typologically diverse African languages using African-centric multilingual encoder (AfroXLM-R). Addresses challenges in measuring MT progress for under-resourced African languages.

description: Congolese Swahili-French bidirectional neural machine translation for humanitarian response. Created 25,302-sentence parallel corpus for training.
